description: A write to a source is observable at every transitive depth, and MUST be replayed against every context a binding ships. The chain here is four deep (topic -> a -> b -> c) because a two-link chain cannot distinguish a correct implementation from one that propagates exactly one level, and a one-level-only defect is the natural failure mode: an invalidation handler that marks its own node stale but never notifies its own dependents. This fixture asserts only what a caller can observe -- the value read at each depth after a source write -- and deliberately does NOT assert how staleness is represented. A pull-based binding may mark nothing at all and let each read refresh its dependencies recursively; an eager binding may mark the whole cone at write time. Both conform, because the contract is that a read observes values consistent with the current sources (glitch-freedom), not that any particular node was flagged. What neither may do is serve a stale cached value at depth. Known hazard motivating this fixture: a binding whose *asynchronous* context tracks staleness by revision counters and in-flight state can break the pull chain that makes the lazy strategy work, so a chain that refreshes correctly in the synchronous context silently stops refreshing in the async one. That is why the replay-against-every-context requirement is part of this fixture rather than a note elsewhere -- a binding that replays it only against its default context can pass while its async path is broken (#lzdartobservercow, lazily-dart async cascade).

expect.note: 1 + 10 + 100 + 1000 — establishes the cached value at every depth before any write

the discriminating step. Depth 1 (a) refreshes even under a one-level-only implementation; depth 2 (b) and depth 3 (c) do not. A binding serving 111 or 1011 here is propagating a bounded number of levels rather than reaching the whole cone.
re-read is stable — refreshing must not be a one-shot that leaves the node stale for the next reader
the == store-guard still applies at depth: an equal write is not a write, and nothing recomputes
a second real write reaches depth 3 again — the first cascade must not have consumed the edges permanently
